annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

tools.description: Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.

body.description: Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

state_reason.description: The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.

tools.name: close_issue

tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization

message.description: The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols

owners.description: Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')

tools.name: mention_owners

content.text: {"number":57948,"title":"[KeyVault] Docs: Remove orphaned ToC entry in Secrets MigrationGuide after sharelink retirement","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets` **Service directory:** `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/` **Triggered by:** PR #57907 by `@jsquire` ## What Changed PR #57907 retired the `sharelink` stand-alone sample (deprecated KV-managed storage accounts feature) and updated stale links in `MigrationGuide.md`. However, the Table of Contents entry for "Managing shared access signatures" was left in place despite the corresponding section body being absent from the document. ## Gaps Found - `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/MigrationGuide.md` contains a Table of Contents link `[Managing shared access signatures](#managing-shared-access-signatures)` (line 19) that points to a section that does not exist in the document body. This creates a broken anchor link and misleads readers into expecting documentation for a deprecated feature. - No mention anywhere in the guide that the KV-managed storage accounts / SAS feature has been deprecated, which could confuse users migrating from `Microsoft.Azure.KeyVault` who used that feature. 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/MigrationGuide.md` **Action:** Edit Two changes are needed in this file: **Change 1 — Remove the orphaned ToC entry** (around line 19): Remove this line from the Table of Contents: ```markdown - [Managing shared access signatures](#managing-shared-access-signatures) ``` **Change 2 — Add a deprecation note for managed storage / SAS** after the "Deleting secrets" section and before the "Additional samples" section. Insert the following new section: ```markdown ### Managing shared access signatures The Azure Key Vault managed storage accounts feature — including SAS definition management — has been [deprecated]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) Microsoft recommends storing SAS tokens or storage account keys directly as Key Vault secrets instead of using the managed storage accounts feature. If you were previously using `KeyVaultClient` methods such as `SetSasDefinitionAsync`, `GetSasDefinitionAsync`, `GetSasDefinitionsAsync`, or `RegenerateStorageAccountKeyAsync` from `Microsoft.Azure.KeyVault`, you should migrate to managing your storage credentials manually and storing them as ordinary Key Vault secrets via `SecretClient.SetSecretAsync`. For more information, see [Manage storage account keys with Key Vault and the Azure CLI (legacy)]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No new snippet infrastructure is needed for this change — the new section is prose-only with no embedded code samples. ### Step 3: Verify README structure No README changes are required; this is a MigrationGuide-only fix. Verify `MigrationGuide.md` sections remain in correct order: 1. Migration benefits ✅ 2. General changes ✅ - Package and namespaces - Separate clients - Client constructors - Setting secrets - Getting secrets - Listing secrets - Listing secret versions - Deleting secrets - **Managing shared access signatures** ← new prose section to add 3. Additional samples ✅ 4. Support ✅ ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 keyvault` — confirm no README snippet regressions 4. `dotnet format sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24157283885/agentic_workflow) · ● 556.8K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57948","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-08T20:51:54Z","updated_at":"2026-04-08T20:51:54Z"}

Insert the following new section: ```markdown ### Managing shared access signatures The Azure Key Vault managed storage accounts feature — including SAS definition management — has been [deprecated]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) Microsoft recommends storing SAS tokens or storage account keys directly as Key Vault secrets instead of using the managed storage accounts feature. If you were previously using `KeyVaultClient` methods such as `SetSasDefinitionAsync`, `GetSasDefinitionAsync`, `GetSasDefinitionsAsync`, or `RegenerateStorageAccountKeyAsync` from `Microsoft.Azure.KeyVault`, you should migrate to managing your storage credentials manually and storing them as ordinary Key Vault secrets via `SecretClient.SetSecretAsync`. General changes ✅ - Package and namespaces - Separate clients - Client constructors - Setting secrets - Getting secrets - Listing secrets - Listing secret versions - Deleting secrets - **Managing shared access signatures** ← new prose section to add 3. Additional samples ✅ 4. Support ✅ ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 keyvault` — confirm no README snippet regressions 4. `dotnet format sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24157283885/agentic_workflow) · ● 556.8K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57948","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-08T20:51:54Z","updated_at":"2026-04-08T20:51:54Z"}

{"total_count":4,"incomplete_results":false,"items":[{"id":1919008716,"number":39032,"state":"closed","state_reason":"completed","locked":true,"title":"Issue connecting to my account when using IAzureClientFactory to retrieve KeyVault data","body":"### Library name and version

Azure.Security.KeyVault

### Query/Question

Hello all!

### Context
I have an API that connects to a `KeyVault` using a `SecretClient` and a `CertificateClient` to retrieve specific data. I am authorized to access the `KeyVault`, and I possess the necessary permissions.

I have an older Microsoft account that offers both Personal and Work options, with the Work option being utilized for the Azure Portal.

### Problem
#### Method 1:
When I try to access to the `KeyVault` with the `IAzureClientFactory` interface configuration:
In `Program.cs`:
```
services.AddAzureClients(async clientBuilder =>
{
    clientBuilder.AddCertificateClient(new Uri("")).WithName("Name");
});
```

Constructor:
```
public Class1(IAzureClientFactory<CertificateClient> certificateClientFactory)
{
   certificateClient = certificateClientFactory.CreateClient("Name");
}
```

I get this error, and there is no way to retrieve data from this `KeyVault`
![image](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/assets/4748759/ed84c9c4-5348-4074-88d0-4574bc9b5e84)

**BUT**

#### Method 2:
If I don't use dependency injection and I create a new client I don't get an error, the connection is made and the data is retrieved. 

Constructor:
```
public Class1()
{
    certificateClient = new CertificateClient(new Uri("Uri"), new DefaultAzureCredential());
}
```

![image](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/assets/4748759/bab074df-e7e7-4c61-b84d-48c7c32849b4)


**My question is:**
Is there a distinction between Method 1 and Method 2? Perhaps the credentials utilized in the dependency injection are not the `DefaultAzureCredential()`, which might explain why it's unable to retrieve the correct user. If this is indeed a bug, kindly inform me, and I will file a report for it. Any response or feedback is greatly appreciated. If you require additional information, please don't hesitate to inform me.

### Environment

Windows 11 Enterprise Insider (Dev channel)

.NET SDK: 
- Version:   8.0.100-rc.1.23455.8
- Commit:    e14caf947f

IDE and version: 
- Microsoft Visual Studio Professional 2022 (64-bit) - Preview

we cache unwrapkeyasync?","body":"

Any C# example will do.

support for JWE to Azure.Security.KeyVault 4.1+ or guidance on safely rolling keys","body":"**Library or service name.**
Azure.Security.KeyVault
 
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Key Vault has features that make creating new versions of certificates and secrets easy.  However, it order to be able to safely expire a keys or certificate, you need to be able to keep track of which keys you have used to encrypt and sign data you already have.  From my research it would seem that JWE offers this ability.  It seems that all of the base components already exist in the Key Vault client libraries so it would be possible to create your own implementation, but this is an area where it would be easy to make a mistake, so having a dependable implementation would be beneficial.

If using JWE in this way is a terrible idea, then some documented guidance on how to deal with rolling keys would be a good alternative.

KeyVault libraries - data cryptography","body":"### Library name and version

Microsoft.Azure.KeyVault.* 4.x.x

### Query/Question

Hi all!

Do KeyVault libraries (Microsoft.Azure.KeyVault.* or Azure.Security.KeyVault.*) support data cryptography? When my application gets or sets keys (and values), inside library, are data stored in memory with cryptography or clear text?

Scenario: I am evaluating best practices to keep secrets secure to avoid leaks in Kubernetes Pods. My application runs on AKS.

Thanks.

### Environment

.NET Core 3.1 and 5, AKS Linux Containers (Alpine Linux)

arguments.message: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

arguments.body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This bot-filed issue identifies a documentation gap in `Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/MigrationGuide.md` — a broken Table of Contents anchor link (`#managing-shared-access-signatures`) and missing deprecation notice for the KV-managed storage accounts feature, introduced after PR #57907 retired the sharelink sample.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets` (no version specified)
**Affected API:** `MigrationGuide.md` — Table of Contents entry at line 19
**Scenarios:**
- Orphaned ToC entry `[Managing shared access signatures](#managing-shared-access-signatures)` points to a non-existent anchor, creating a broken link
- No deprecation note explaining that KV-managed storage accounts / SAS has been deprecated, potentially confusing users migrating from `Microsoft.Azure.KeyVault`

**Root ask:** Remove the orphaned ToC entry and add a prose deprecation note for the managed storage / SAS feature in `MigrationGuide.md`.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

The issue is purely documentation. The broken anchor can be verified by opening `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/MigrationGuide.md` and confirming that line 19 contains a ToC link with no matching `### Managing shared access signatures` heading in the document body.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Open `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/MigrationGuide.md` and confirm line 19 contains the orphaned `[Managing shared access signatures]` ToC entry.
2. Verify no `### Managing shared access signatures` section exists in the document body.
3. Remove the orphaned ToC line and add a deprecation note section before "Additional samples".
4. Run `dotnet build sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/` and `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 keyvault` to confirm no regressions.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Client` — `Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets` is a data-plane client library (not management or provisioning)
- **Service:** `KeyVault` — Issue explicitly references `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/`; quality reference issues #34916 and #15596 confirm this label pattern
- **Confidence:** High — Package and service directory are explicitly named in the issue body; category and service are unambiguous; label pattern validated against quality reference issues
</details>

<details>
<summary>👥 Owner Routing</summary>

- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %KeyVault` (line 333) — `%KeyVault` matches the predicted service label; this is the only ServiceLabel entry for KeyVault in CODEOWNERS
- **AzureSdkOwners:** none listed
